picks.reason: deGrom is the best starter in this game, but Texas gives back part of that edge with a more compromised bullpen and only a modest platoon advantage against Pérez. My side number lands too close to the market to clear the 3.5-point gate. The total also sits near my projection in a run-suppressing park, and roof status matters enough here that I do not want exposure this early.

Bibee’s underlying skill and recent shape are better than Fedde’s, and Cleveland also has the cleaner bullpen outlook with Chicago’s leverage group heavily thinned. The problem is Cleveland’s weak run-production baseline versus righties, which keeps my fair price short of a bet threshold. I project the total slightly above 8.5 because Fedde and the taxed White Sox pen can leak late runs, but not by enough to stake it.
picks.data_gap: confirmed lineups; exact wind effect at first pitch

picks.reason: Boston has the better starter, the far better platoon split versus a lefty, and the more trustworthy bullpen, while Colorado’s lineup profile versus lefties is poor. Even so, Coors inflates variance enough that I will not force a moneyline at this favorite price. My total projection is above 11, but Coors is exactly the kind of wide-distribution environment where I want more cushion before betting.

Soriano is the better and more stable starter, but the LAA side price already reflects that and Gibson’s small-sample volatility suppresses my confidence rather than creating a clean edge. Both offenses profile well enough to tempt an over, yet that total case is too dependent on the unstable Gibson input to clear my raised small-sample threshold. This is a classic pass on uncertainty rather than a bet on a noisy mean.

Skubal is the best arm on the slate tier, but New York’s offense versus lefties and Weathers’ solid underlying skill keep Detroit from reaching a playable side gap at the current number. My total projection sits essentially on the market after accounting for both starters and two decent late-game relief groups. With no meaningful price separation, this game stays off the card.
picks.data_gap: confirmed lineups; any weather downgrade closer to first pitch

picks.reason: Tampa has the better overall game-state because Kansas City’s bullpen is badly depleted, but the Rays’ weak split versus lefties keeps the moneyline from clearing my side gate. The over is the more interesting angle because KC has been hitting and its tired bullpen can turn a five-inning game into an eight-run game quickly. Still, my adjusted projection does not quite get far enough past 8.0 to bet.

The starters are close, both offenses grade well versus righties, and the bullpen split cuts opposite ways with Seattle stronger overall but Pittsburgh likely more vulnerable late. That leaves me without a clean side separator, especially with the best Seattle moneyline price unavailable due to suspect data. My total projection is only a touch above market once PNC’s run suppression is applied, so I pass both markets.

picks.reason: I do not have enough separation to lay the Phillies price because Nola’s true level is better than his ERA, but not enough better to create a strong side number against a live Washington offense. The total is cleaner: both teams project for offense versus right-handed pitching, Mikolas is the weaker starter, Washington’s bullpen is light in setup depth, and the park/weather combo is favorable. My regressed total lands around 10.4, which is enough to play over 9.5 at the available price.

Toronto is fairly priced to slightly expensive for me because Houston’s offense versus righties is strong enough to keep the side from becoming a play even with the Astros’ bullpen in rough shape. The total is the better angle: Burrows is a weak baseline starter, Yesavage’s recent shape has slipped, Houston’s relief availability is poor, and the offensive splits point toward sustained scoring chances on both sides. My adjusted projection is 9.7, giving the over a clear cushion above 8.5.

Milwaukee is the better team, but Cincinnati’s strong split versus left-handed pitching keeps the side market close enough to fair that I will not pay Brewers chalk. The total has multiple independent drivers: Milwaukee’s elite offense versus a weak righty, Cincinnati’s capable split versus lefties, two starters whose estimators are worse than their surface lines, and a warm Great American environment that amplifies homer risk. My regressed projection is 10.3, so over 9.0 at plus money is a clear bet.

The Dodgers have the better full-team profile, but Joe Ryan is good enough to keep the moneyline from being attractive at a heavy favorite price. The total is lower than a pure run-baseline model would suggest because both offenses are dangerous, yet two strong starters and a modest park keep me from forcing the over. I make it only slightly above market after regression, so it stays a lean.

picks.reason: Jump’s small body of work and Mahle’s shaky surface line create a tempting anti-SF case, but San Francisco’s much fresher bullpen and home edge keep the side close to market. The total is tricky because both offenses have useful platoon paths, yet Oracle’s weather and run suppression pull hard the other way. With distribution width elevated by the young starter and conflicting signals, I stay out.
